provider-token-signal.guard: cd /Users/nix/dev/node/adhd && npx nx build agent-mcp && npx nx build agent-mcp-types && grep -rq 'TokenUsage' dist/packages/ai/agent-mcp-types/
provider-token-signal.notes: TokenUsage is defined in packages/ai/agent-mcp-types/src/domain.ts and imported from @adhd/agent-mcp-types into providers/types.ts — not defined locally in providers/types.ts. hooks.ts already imports from ./domain.js so this avoids a cross-package import. OpenAI SDK response.usage has prompt_tokens/completion_tokens; Anthropic has input_tokens/output_tokens. claudecli returns undefined — correct and expected. Do not try to extract tokens from the CLI subprocess.

hook-token-payload.guard: cd /Users/nix/dev/node/adhd && npx nx build agent-mcp && npx nx build agent-mcp-types && grep -n 'tokenUsage' dist/packages/ai/agent-mcp-types/index.d.ts | grep -q 'TokenUsage'
hook-token-payload.notes: PostModelResponsePayload is defined in agent-mcp-types. Orchestrator reads providerResponse.usage and passes it as tokenUsage in the hook emit. The field is optional — downstream consumers must handle undefined (claudecli, failed calls). Also: ExecutionContext gains rootTaskId; TaskStartPayload gains rootTaskId; task:start emit wires it through; task:failed/task:cancelled change from void to await; tools/task.ts derives rootTaskId at task-creation time from callerContext.

audit-foundation.notes: Audit is read-only. Most likely failures: (1) TokenUsage type not exported from agent-mcp-types index, (2) orchestrator passes undefined where tokenUsage is expected to be an object.

usage-schema.guard: cd /Users/nix/dev/node/adhd && grep -n 'task_usage' packages/ai/agent-mcp/src/db/schema.ts | grep -q 'sqliteTable' && ls packages/ai/agent-mcp/drizzle/ | grep -q 'task.usage'
usage-schema.notes: Run 'npm run db:generate' from packages/ai/agent-mcp/ to produce the migration file after editing schema.ts. The migration filename will contain the date — that is fine. Do NOT manually write the SQL file.

usage-plugin.guard: cd /Users/nix/dev/node/adhd && npx nx build agent-mcp && grep -rn 'UsagePlugin' dist/packages/ai/agent-mcp/src/ | grep -q 'install'
usage-plugin.notes: UsagePlugin must not throw — it is observational. Flush happens on task:completed/failed/cancelled. The in-memory accumulation map is keyed by taskId. Ephemeral tasks (agent_name mode) DO get flushed — the orchestrator always fires task:completed regardless of session mode. The resulting task_usage row will have no corresponding tasks row, which is expected and correct.

usage-query-tool.guard: cd /Users/nix/dev/node/adhd && npx nx build agent-mcp && grep -n 'usage_query' dist/packages/ai/agent-mcp/src/server.js | grep -q 'usage_query'
usage-query-tool.notes: New MCP tool is named 'usage_query'. Also rename the existing 'usage' guide tool to 'guide' in server.ts (same state, already mutates server.ts). Follows the existing pattern in server.ts: add to inProcessDescriptors, add case to inProcessHandler switch, add ListTools entry, add CallTool case. The query layer belongs in tools/usage.ts; validation schemas in validation/usage.ts. Also enriches resultTool() and sync taskTool() with usage (direct + subtree) via buildTaskUsageReport() — see [dod.2] and context notes.

audit-final.notes: The live data check requires LM Studio running with LMSTUDIO_API_KEY and LMSTUDIO_BASE_URL set. If not available, skip only the live check and note it — all static checks must pass.

docs-and-publish.notes: Update GAPS.md (item #4 implemented), ROADMAP.md (Phase 1 item #2 complete), INSTALL.md + README.md (task_usage tool documented). Bump version 0.0.4→0.0.5. Rebuild. Publish: npm publish dist/packages/ai/agent-mcp --access public

acceptance-test.notes: Dispatch a zero-context subagent against the production @adhd/agent-mcp npm package. The prompt must not mention 'usage', 'token', 'task_usage', or any hint about where usage data lives. The subagent must independently discover and report sub-task token counts from a ≥2-hop recursive task. Verify numbers against raw SQLite DB. Write acceptance-evidence.md with VERIFIED_MATCH.
